steps.title: Identify the central clue
steps.content: The passage sets up a contrast with “however”—Argerich's performances appear spontaneous and relaxed, but this appearance is actually caused by tremendous preparation. The passage's main point is resolving this apparent contradiction.

steps.title: Match the clue to the choices
steps.content: Choice B captures the passage's essential argument: Argerich's performances look effortless because she invests tremendous preparation. The other choices fail to identify the central point—they focus on piece selection, music preferences, or an approach limited to unfamiliar pieces, when the passage is about preparation creating the look of ease. That matches choice B — B is correct.
